title: Add the mev path dep + `bastion validate-brain` (6-way flag dispatch)
description:
- `crates/bastion/Cargo.toml`: add `mev = { path = "../mev" }` to [dependencies]. Confirm the workspace builds with mev pulled in (do NOT modify anything under ../mev).
- New module `crates/bastion/src/brainval/mod.rs` with PURE helpers: `report_to_exit_code(&mev::Report) -> u8` (1 when `report.is_failure()`, else 0), `render_human(&mev::Report) -> String` (a summary line + one line per diagnostic: severity, file, locator, message), and a `--json` path that serializes `mev::JsonReport::new("brain", root, &report).to_json()`. Register `mod brainval;` in `main.rs`.
- `crates/bastion/src/cli.rs`: declare `Commands::ValidateBrain { path: PathBuf (default "."), sync: bool, graph: bool, state: bool, links: bool, structure: bool }`, mirroring mev's clap args/help.
- `crates/bastion/src/main.rs`: add the name-mapper entry (`ValidateBrain { .. } => "validate-brain"`) and a dispatch arm that selects the mev fn by mev's precedence `--links > --structure > --state > --graph > --sync > base` — calling `mev::validate_brain_links` / `_structure` / `_state` / `_graph` / `_sync` / `mev::validate_brain` respectively — then renders via brainval (human or `--json`) and sets the process exit code from `report_to_exit_code`.
- Unit-test the pure selection + exit-code + `--json` rendering (flag combo → chosen fn label; `Report` with an error → exit 1; empty `Report` → exit 0). Smoke-test `bastion validate-brain` against the brain corpus; record in Notes.
acceptance_criteria: Cargo.toml declares mev = { path = "../mev" } and the workspace builds; nothing under ../mev changed
bastion validate-brain honors mev's flag precedence and calls the matching mev::validate_brain* fn
--json output is byte-identical to `mev validate-brain … --json`; exit code is 1 on failure else 0
flag→fn selection and exit-code logic are unit-tested

title: Add `bastion manifest`, `graph`, and `emit-state` (mev pass-throughs)
description:
- `crates/bastion/src/cli.rs`: declare `Commands::Manifest { path (default "."), pretty: bool }`, `Commands::Graph { path (default ".") }`, and `Commands::EmitState { path (default "."), write: bool }`, mirroring mev's clap args/help.
- `crates/bastion/src/brainval/mod.rs`: add handlers calling `mev::manifest_brain(root)` (serialize `Manifest` to stdout — compact by default, pretty with `--pretty`, matching mev's manifest output), `mev::graph_brain(root)` (serialize `GraphExport` to stdout), and `mev::emit_state(root, write)` (render the returned `Report` via the existing brainval helpers; dry-run is the default, `--write` applies).
- `crates/bastion/src/main.rs`: add name-mapper entries + dispatch arms for the three commands.
- Unit-test the pure output selection (pretty vs compact manifest serialization; emit-state exit code from its `Report`). Smoke-test each against the brain corpus vs the equivalent `mev` subcommand; record in Notes.
acceptance_criteria: bastion manifest [--pretty] output matches `mev manifest [--pretty]` on the brain corpus
bastion graph output matches `mev`'s graph export JSON
bastion emit-state defaults to dry-run and reports the same planned actions as `mev emit-state`; --write applies
pretty/compact selection + emit-state exit code are unit-tested

title: Add `bastion view` / `edit` over bella-engine
description:
- `crates/bastion/src/cli.rs`: declare `Commands::View { path: PathBuf }` and `Commands::Edit { path: PathBuf }`.
- New module `crates/bastion/src/docview/mod.rs`: `view(path)` opens the file through bella-engine's document viewer; `edit(path)` opens it in bella's editor. FIRST confirm the correct entrypoint: `bella_engine::markdown::render`/`render_with_edit` return a `Rendered` buffer (not an interactive loop) — check whether bella-engine exposes a higher-level interactive `run`/`app` open; if not, mirror bella's own binary (`../../../bella` crate `bella`, its `main.rs`) app loop. Keep DB-free (D4). Record the resolved entrypoint in tasks.md §Notes.
- `crates/bastion/src/main.rs`: name-mapper entries + dispatch arms for `View`/`Edit`.
- Unit-test whatever pure logic exists (e.g. path validation / not-found handling); smoke-test the interactive open and record in Notes. Do NOT modify any bella source.
acceptance_criteria: bastion view <file> renders a markdown document via bella-engine
bastion edit <file> opens the document in bella's editor via bella-engine
no file under ../../../bella is modified; the resolved bella entrypoint is recorded in Notes
path handling (e.g. missing file) is covered and degrades cleanly

title: Validate + parity smoke-test
description: Run the Validation Commands from the workspace root: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo build --release`. Then run the parity smoke-tests from the brain root and record results in tasks.md §Notes: `bastion validate-brain --json` vs `mev validate-brain --json` (byte-identical); `bastion manifest` vs `mev manifest`; `bastion graph` vs `mev`'s graph export; `bastion emit-state` (dry-run) vs `mev emit-state` (same planned actions). Confirm combined test count is not lower than before the block.
acceptance_criteria: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo build --release all pass at the workspace root
bastion's brain subcommands produce output matching the equivalent mev subcommand (json byte-identical), recorded in Notes
combined workspace test count is not lower than before the block
